title: Fix Procedure conversation history loading in dashboard
description:
## Intent
Restore conversation history visibility for procedure runs in dashboard conversation viewer when generated model helpers are missing from the Amplify client.

## Scope
- Harden  query path with GraphQL fallback for chat sessions/messages by .
- Guard subscription setup when model  helpers are unavailable.
- Preserve existing rendering/filtering behavior.

## Definition of Done
- Opening a procedure run no longer throws on undefined  / .
- Existing history loads via fallback and renders in UI.
- No regression for environments where generated model helpers exist.
- Tests added/updated for fallback behavior where practical.

comments.author: ryan
comments.text:
Decision update: removing model-helper fallback logic in `conversation-viewer.tsx` per project direction.

New approach is single-path and explicit:
- load chat sessions/messages via direct GraphQL index queries
- subscribe via GraphQL `onCreateChatSession`/`onCreateChatMessage` with procedureId filter
- no generated-model helper dependency and no compatibility branches.

comments.author: ryan
comments.text:
Progress: removed generated-model fallback branches from conversation viewer.

Current implementation is single-path GraphQL for list + subscriptions keyed by procedureId.

comments.author: ryan
comments.text: Updated repo guidance in `AGENTS.md` with explicit implementation discipline: no fallback/compatibility branches by default; prefer one canonical path and explicit diagnostics.

comments.author: ryan
comments.text:
Starting implementation of deterministic Node toolchain plan:
- pin repo/dashboard to v20.17.0
- document `nvm use` requirement to avoid Homebrew Node drift
- clear stale typecheck processes
- re-run checks with explicit v20.17.0 node/npm binaries.

comments.author: ryan
comments.text: Adjustment after deployment constraint review: aligning Node pin to the existing Amplify-linked baseline (`18.19.0`) instead of `20.17.0`. Will update pin files/docs accordingly, install/use 18.19 locally, and rerun verification.

comments.author: ryan
comments.text:
Implemented Node toolchain alignment to deployment baseline:
- set root `.nvmrc` to 18.19.0
- restored `dashboard/.nvmrc` to 18.19.0
- added explicit Node runtime guidance in AGENTS.md and CONTRIBUTING_AGENT.md (require `nvm use`, avoid Homebrew Node)
- installed local Node v18.19.0 via nvm

Verification:
- `which node/npm` resolves to `~/.nvm/versions/node/v18.19.0/bin/*` when PATH is prefixed
- `node -v` => v18.19.0, `npm -v` => 10.2.3
- dashboard typecheck passes when run with explicit v18 node + npm-cli and PATH prefixed

Notes:
- targeted ESLint check for `components/ui/conversation-viewer.tsx` still fails due existing ajv/eslint toolchain issue (`Cannot set properties of undefined (setting defaultMeta)`), independent of Node pin
- prior stale typecheck processes remain in uninterruptible kernel sleep (`UE/UEs`) and are not killable even with SIGKILL.

comments.author: ryan
comments.text: Starting implementation: add first-class Tactus trace emission and Plexus trace sink mapping so procedure runs persist ChatMessage records (not just ChatSession). Will wire runtime->DSPy agent->Plexus adapter, then add tests and validate with procedure dry-run path.

comments.author: ryan
comments.text: Continuing implementation: validating trace persistence end-to-end from Tactus runtime into Plexus chat session/message records, then fixing remaining run failures.

comments.author: ryan
comments.text:
Implemented and verified Tactus trace persistence end-to-end. Added TraceEvent/TraceSink protocol in Tactus, wired runtime start/flush/end lifecycle, added PlexusTraceSink adapter to ProcedureChatRecorder, and validated records in API (sessions/messages by procedureId). Fixed duplicate trace emission bug in Tactus DSPy agent (user/assistant/tool events were being enqueued multiple times in streaming path). Verified with fresh runs: smoke run now completes and produces 1 user + 1 assistant message; Create Scorecard dry-run run_id=cac0c1c3-d112-4fbe-bc02-99505da1c750 completes and records 12 messages in chat history.

Remaining runtime quality issue discovered during dry-run: MCP tool arg-shape mismatch for log_message (unexpected keyword argument message) still triggers fallback invocation path and can degrade drafted guideline content. Next fix is to normalize DSPy->MCP tool argument invocation so wrappers receive canonical args without fallback coercion.

comments.author: ryan
comments.text:
Additional fixes completed after trace wiring: fixed MCP tool schema export in EmbeddedMCPServer so ProcedureMCPClient.list_tools returns real parameter schemas (previously properties were empty, causing Tactus adapter to build empty arg models and drop tool args). Added schema extraction from function signatures in mcp_transport and added a regression test that verifies plexus_guidelines_validate exposes guidelines_markdown in its input schema.

Also removed executor-level DSPy monkey patch fallback and moved invocation-shape handling into Tactus DSPyAgentHandle._execute_tool with deterministic call attempts. Verified adapter model now includes guidelines_markdown and tool calls pass args through to MCP.

comments.author: ryan
comments.text: Procedure conversation history loading fixed in dashboard.
